jurisdiction: City of Toronto / Province of Ontario
disclaimer: Heuristic site-pack checks for demo briefing — not a zoning opinion, survey, or legal advice. Confirm with City Planning / a qualified code professional.

zoning_district.note: Default overlay for St. Lawrence / Harbourfront demo parcels. Real designation must be confirmed on the City of Toronto Zoning By-law Map.

site_overlays.parking_note: Downtown Toronto largely uses parking maximums; this pack still flags hospitality stall demand as a site-plan discussion item.
site_overlays.source_label: Site overlay pack: downtown CR heuristic (height / setbacks / FSI / angular plane)

assumptions.note: Storey height and lot depth are labelled estimates. sqft_per_room mirrors model/innsight_model/benchmarks.py SQFT_PER_ROOM so the compliance GFA always matches the sim's floor area.

building_code.clause: OBC Part 3 — Fire Protection, Occupant Safety and Accessibility (Group C / hospitality occupancy for purpose-built lodging)
building_code.note: Boutique and tower hospitality are typically Part 3; small homestays may sit closer to Part 9 depending on size and use.

building_code.clause: OBC amendments permitting encapsulated mass timber construction within stated height/area limits
building_code.note: Mass timber Option B is checked against this pack limit; detailing (fire, acoustic) still adds design cost.

building_code.clause: AODA / OBC barrier-free design — accessible guest rooms and public areas
building_code.note: Room count drives accessible-suite share discussions; not auto-sized in this demo.

energy_and_climate.clause: OBC energy efficiency provisions for new buildings
energy_and_climate.note: New buildings must meet current OBC energy provisions; Toronto Green Standard (TGS) may apply for site plan applications.

energy_and_climate.clause: Toronto Green Standard (TGS) — energy, GHG, and resilience requirements on site-plan pathway
energy_and_climate.note: Electrification and lower embodied carbon favour Option B when TGS applies.

energy_and_climate.clause: Gas-fired central plant — currently code-compliant; carbon-price and future electrification policy exposure

hospitality_ops.clause: Toronto STR / lodging tax + HST on accommodation revenue
hospitality_ops.note: Lodging revenue typically attracts HST and local accommodation tax; structure does not change tax, but scale does.

hospitality_ops.clause: City of Toronto short-term rental bylaw / licensing (homestay / principal-residence pathways)
hospitality_ops.note: Homestay operators may need STR registration; purpose-built hotels follow commercial lodging rules.

building_type_notes.homestay: Small operator short-term rental rules and licensing may apply; check Toronto STR bylaw and provincial hotel tax if advertising as lodging.
building_type_notes.boutique: Purpose-built hotel typically under Ontario Building Code Part 3; hospitality occupancy classification.
building_type_notes.tower: High-rise Part 3; additional high-building measures and egress.

sources.label: City of Toronto Zoning By-law 569-2013 (overlay pack is heuristic, not parcel-matched)
Ontario Building Code / EMTC guidance (height limit estimate in pack)
